title: FTMO Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026)
meta_title: FTMO Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026) | TradingToolsHub
meta_description: FTMO Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026). FTMO (4.5★, $155/mo). Step-by-step setup, features walkthrough, and pro tips. Updated 2026.
excerpt: Complete guide to setting up and using FTMO — from account creation to pro-level tips.
content:
What is FTMO?

FTMO is a proprietary trading firm based in Prague, Czech Republic, that has been offering funded trading accounts since 2015. Instead of trading your own capital, FTMO evaluates your trading skills through a structured challenge process and provides you with a funded account—up to $200,000—if you pass their two-phase assessment. You keep 90% of the profits you generate, with FTMO retaining 10%. Rated 4.5/5 by traders, FTMO is the most established prop firm in the industry, supporting traders on MT4, MT5, cTrader, and DXtrade platforms. It's built for forex, crypto, stocks, and futures traders with a proven strategy who want access to significant capital without risking their own money.

How to Create Your FTMO Account

Getting started with FTMO takes about 10-15 minutes. Here's the process:

  • Visit FTMO.com and click "Start Trading": You'll land on the account creation page. Choose whether you're a new or returning trader.
  • Enter your email and create a password: Use a strong password—you'll be accessing real trading platforms through this account. Confirm your email address via the link they send.
  • Complete your profile: Provide your full name, country of residence, and date of birth. FTMO is regulated in the EU, so age verification is required.
  • Select your challenge: Choose your starting challenge tier: $10K, $25K, $50K, $100K, or $200K. First-time traders should start with the Free Trial to test the platform without spending money.
  • Accept terms and conditions: Review FTMO's rules, including the 5% daily drawdown limit, 10% maximum loss requirement, and minimum 20-trade requirement for the Phase 1 evaluation.
  • Verify your identity: For challenges costing $155 or more, FTMO requires identity verification. Upload a photo ID and a proof of residence (utility bill or bank statement). This typically completes within 24 hours.
  • Access your trading environment: Once verified, you'll receive login credentials for your MT4/MT5 simulator environment and a link to download the trading platform.

Estimated setup time: 15-30 minutes (faster if documents are ready; verification adds 24 hours).

Setting Up FTMO for the First Time

After logging in, you'll see the FTMO Dashboard—your control center for managing challenges and tracking performance.

Dashboard Layout: The dashboard displays your current challenge status, account balance, equity, profit/loss, and daily drawdown percentage in real time. A progress bar shows how close you are to completing Phase 1 of your evaluation.

Key settings to configure immediately:

  • Connect your broker: FTMO provides MT4 and MT5 simulators, but you can also trade using your own broker's platform if it's supported. Copy your account login credentials and server address from FTMO's dashboard into your trading platform. Demo accounts sync live, showing real-time P&L.
  • Enable two-factor authentication: Found in Account Settings, this protects your funded account from unauthorized access.
  • Set your risk parameters: FTMO enforces these automatically, but it's critical you understand them. Your daily loss limit is 5% of the account size. If you hit this, your challenge ends. Your maximum loss across the entire challenge is 10%.
  • Review the rule set: Open the "Rules" section from your dashboard. The 5% daily drawdown rule is the most important—one losing day can eliminate you. The minimum 20-trade requirement in Phase 1 ensures you have a sufficient sample size to prove consistency.
  • Configure notifications: Enable email and push notifications (via the mobile app) to alert you when you're approaching drawdown limits or when phases change.

Essential Features You Should Know

1. Paper Trading & Simulated Accounts: FTMO's entire evaluation happens on simulated trading—your trades don't hit real markets. This removes slippage and real-world execution delays, but it also means you'll adjust to live trading speed and volume when you graduate. Use the simulator to test your strategy risk-free before spending on a challenge.

2. Performance Analytics: FTMO's built-in analytics dashboard tracks 15+ metrics: win rate, profit factor, average trade duration, largest winning/losing trade, and equity curve visualization. This data rivals standalone journaling apps like Thinkorswim. Export your data as CSV for deeper analysis in Excel or Python.

3. Journaling Tools: After each trade, log your reasoning directly in FTMO's journal—why you entered, what triggered your exit, and what you'd do differently. This forced reflection is critical for improving consistency. Traders who journal show 30%+ better pass rates than those who don't.

4. Mobile App: FTMO's iOS and Android apps let you monitor your account from anywhere. You can't trade on mobile, but you can track your equity, drawdown %, and notifications in real time. The app is essential if you're trading during off-hours or managing multiple challenges.

5. Automated Trading & EA Support: FTMO supports Expert Advisors (EAs) and algorithmic strategies on MT4/MT5. Upload your EA, backtest it, and let it trade autonomously during your challenge. Algo traders make up roughly 30% of FTMO's user base—the platform is designed for them.

6. Risk Management Tools: FTMO automatically enforces position sizing limits based on your account size and risk rules. You can't override these, which protects overlevered traders from themselves. The system calculates max lot size based on your daily loss limit.

7. Education & Resources: FTMO provides webinars, strategy guides, and a knowledge base covering everything from risk management to platform tutorials. New traders should watch the "FTMO Rules Explained" webinar before starting their first challenge.

FTMO Pricing: Which Plan Should You Choose?

Free Trial ($0/month): Start here. You get full access to the trading environment with unlimited retakes. No profit split—just a demo account. Use this to confirm FTMO's platform works for your strategy before spending money.

$10K Challenge ($155/month): Entry-level funded account. Ideal for traders new to prop trading who want to test their strategy with real money on the line (even though it's simulated). The 5% daily drawdown is $500, so you have some margin for error. If you pass both phases, you get access to a $10K funded account earning 90% of profits.

$25K Challenge ($250/month): The most popular tier. $1,250 daily loss limit gives you room to scale positions without constantly hitting drawdown. Recommended for intermediate traders with a proven edge. Monthly profit potential: $2,000–$5,000+ depending on your return rate.

$50K Challenge ($345/month): For traders consistently profitable on lower tiers. $2,500 daily loss limit allows larger position sizes. Jump to this tier only after passing at least one $25K challenge, not as your first attempt. Monthly profit potential: $5,000–$15,000+.

$100K Challenge ($540/month): Advanced traders only. Your challenge fee ($540) will be refunded on your first successful payout, but failing costs real money. $5,000 daily loss limit. This tier makes sense only if you've proven consistent profitability on $25K and $50K accounts first.

$200K Challenge ($1,080/month): The flagship tier for professional traders. Only attempt this after multiple six-figure account successes. Risk the most, earn the most—$200K account with 90% profit split generates serious income for proven traders.

Which tier should you choose? Beginners should start with the Free Trial for 2-4 weeks, then move to the $10K Challenge ($155). Intermediate traders with 6+ months of live trading history and positive expectancy should start at $25K ($250). Advanced traders with multiple profitable months should attempt $50K ($345) or higher. Do not skip tiers—this is the #1 reason traders blow accounts.

Pro Tips for Getting the Most Out of FTMO

1. Use the Free Trial aggressively: You get unlimited retakes on the free trial. Spend 4-6 weeks here testing your exact strategy, entry rules, position sizing, and exit criteria. Once you pass the Free Trial, you'll pass paid tiers faster because your strategy is already proven.

2. Master the 5% daily drawdown rule before you trade: This is FTMO's defining constraint and the #1 killer of funded accounts. If your average trade loses $200 on a $25K account, you can only lose 12-13 trades before hitting the 5% limit ($1,250). Calculate your exact max loss per trade before opening your first position, then stick to it religiously.

3. Trade the first 2 hours after market open: FTMO's simulated data is most accurate during liquid market hours (NY/London overlap for forex). Volume and spreads are realistic, so your edge will show most clearly. Avoid trading the final 30 minutes of the US close when volatility and slippage spike.

4. Leverage your journaling for Phase 2: Phase 1 focuses on meeting minimum trades and staying under drawdown. Phase 2 tests consistency—if you lost money in Phase 1, Phase 2 is your chance to prove that was an outlier. Review your journal entries daily and identify the 3-4 setups with the highest win rate. Trade only those in Phase 2.

5. Automate with EAs if your strategy is discretionary-adjacent: If your setup is mechanical (moving average crossover, support/resistance bounce), build an EA and let it trade 24/5. EAs remove emotion, execute instantly, and backtest faster. FTMO allows EAs on MT4/MT5, and algo traders often pass phases 50% faster than discretionary traders.

6. Scale your challenge fees across months: Don't pay $345 for a $50K challenge immediately. Pay $155 for a $10K challenge, get it passed in 4-6 weeks (most traders take 2-3 attempts), then move to $25K. Spread your challenge fees ($155 + $250 + $345) across 3-4 months instead of one lump sum.

7. Join FTMO's trader community on Telegram/Discord: Successful traders share edge setups, risk management tips, and Phase 2 strategies. The community culture at FTMO is collaborative, not competitive—you're not trading against other FTMO traders, so helping others helps you.

Common FTMO Issues and How to Fix Them

Issue 1: "I hit 5% drawdown on day 3 and got kicked out." This happens because traders don't plan position size before trading. Solution: Before Phase 1 starts, calculate your daily loss limit in dollars (5% of account), then divide by your average loss per trade. Only risk that amount. Use a spreadsheet. Most traders who fail do so because they sized positions for a $100K account while trading the $10K challenge.

Issue 2: "My trades execute differently on FTMO than on my live broker." FTMO uses simulated data that closely mirrors live forex but isn't identical. Slippage is lower, spreads are tighter, and fill speed is instant. Solution: Don't expect your FTMO performance to directly translate to live trading. Treat FTMO as 85-90% accurate. When you get a funded account, you'll adjust within the first week.

Issue 3: "I passed Phase 1 with $2,500 profit but failed Phase 2 after 3 losing trades." Phase 2 tests consistency, not just profitability. A trader with a +$2,500 Phase 1 might have been lucky—five big winners and many small losers. Phase 2 demands steady wins across 20-30 trades. Solution: In Phase 1, don't just chase profit—track your win rate. You need at least 55% win rate to consistently pass Phase 2. Adjust your strategy in Phase 1 if you're close on profit but below 50% win rate.

Issue 4: "The 10% maximum loss on the entire challenge is too harsh." True. A single $5K loss on a $50K account violates the rule. Solution: Never risk more than 2% per trade and enforce a daily loss limit of 3% (FTMO allows 5%, but 3% gives you a 2% buffer). This means on a $50K account, you risk $1,000 per trade maximum and stop trading once you lose $1,500 for the day.

Is FTMO Worth It? Our Verdict

FTMO is worth it if you have a tested, profitable strategy and the discipline to follow strict risk rules. The 90% profit split is industry-leading—competitors like TopStep and Prop Firms X offer 80% splits—and FTMO's challenge fee refund on first payout reduces your cost of entry. The platform is robust: four trading platforms, strong analytics, and a 10-year track record. However, the 85-95% failure rate is real. Most traders don't fail because the platform is bad; they fail because they're undercapitalized, overleveraged, or trading untested strategies. Start with the free trial, prove your edge on $10K, then scale methodically to $25K and beyond. FTMO is not a shortcut to wealth—it's a capital source for traders who've already proven they're profitable. If you're still learning price action, skip FTMO and paper trade for 3-6 months first.
author: TradingToolsHub Editorial
